rationale: The citing paper’s note explicitly states it “complements Cohen (2002),” and the cited 2002 JMKR paper matches that DOI, indicating an intentional link to prior work rather than a generic background mention. Both studies use analgesics scanner data and a random-coefficients demand/pricing framework, making the cited paper a plausible methodological and conceptual foundation for the citing study’s decomposition of retailer pricing mechanisms.
